author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans

ccel.title: NPNF1-11. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles and the Epistle to the Romans

source.title: NPNF1-11. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles and the Epistle to the Romans

title: Homily XI on Rom. vi. 5.

source.title: NPNF1-11. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles and the Epistle to the Romans

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For if we have been planted togetherin the likeness of His death, we shall be also in the likeness of His resurrection.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What I had before occasion to remark, that I mention here too, that he continually digresseth into exhortation, without making any twofold division as he does in the other Epistles, and setting apart the former portion for doctrines, and the latter for the care of moral instruction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here then he does not do so, but blends the latter with the subject throughout, so as to gain it an easy admission.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here then he says there are two mortifyings, and two deaths, and that one is done by Christ in Baptism, and the other it is our duty to effect by earnestness afterwards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that our former sins were buried, came of His gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the remaining dead to sin after baptism must be the work of our own earnestness, however much we find God here also giving us large help.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is not the only thing Baptism has the power to do, to obliterate our former transgressions; for it also secures against subsequent ones.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then in the case of the former, thy contribution was faith that they might be obliterated, so also in those subsequent to this, show thou forth the change in thine aims, that thou mayest not defile thyself again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is this and the like that he is counselling thee when he says, “for if we have been planted together in the likeness of His Death, we shall be also in the likeness of His Resurrection.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do you observe, how he rouses the hearer by leading him straightway up to his Master, and taking great pains to show the strong likeness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This is why he does not say “in death,” lest you should gainsay it, but, “in the likeness of His Death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For our essence itself hath not died, but the man of sins, that is, wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not say, “for if we have been” partakers of “the likeness of His Death;” but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “If we have been planted together,” so, by the mention of planting, giving a hint of the fruit resulting to us from it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as His Body, by being buried in the earth, brought forth as the fruit of it the salvation of the world; thus ours also, being buried in baptism, bore as fruit righteousness, sanctification, adoption, countless blessings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And it will bear also hereafter the gift of the resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then we were buried in water, He in earth, and we in regard to sin, He in regard to His Body, this is why he did not say, “we were planted together in His Death,” but “in the likeness of His Death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For both the one and the other is death, but not that of the same subject.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If then he says, “we have been planted together in His Death, we shall be in that of His Resurrection,” speaking here of the Resurrection which (Gr. be of His Resurrection) is to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For since when he was upon the subject of the Death before, and said, “Know ye not, brethren, that so many of us as were baptized into Christ were baptized into His Death?” he had not made any clear statement about the Resurrection, but only about the way of life after baptism, bidding men walk in newness of life; therefore he here resumes the same subject, and proceeds to foretell to us clearly that Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And that you may know that he is not speaking of that resulting from baptism, but about the other, after saying, “for if we were planted together in the likeness of His Death,” he does not say that we shall be in the likeness of His Resurrection, but we shall belong to the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For to prevent thy saying, and how, if we did not die as He died, are we to rise as He rose? when he mentioned the Death, he did not say, “planted together in the Death,” but, “in the likeness of His Death.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when he mentioned the Resurrection, he did not say, “in the likeness of the Resurrection,” but we shall be “of the Resurrection” itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not say, We have been made, but we shall be, by this word again plainly meaning that Resurrection which has not yet taken place, but will hereafter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then with a view to give credibility to what he says, he points out another Resurrection which is brought about here before that one, that from that which is present thou mayest believe also that which is to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after saying, “we shall be planted together in the Resurrection,” he adds,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Knowing this, that our old man is crucified with Him, that the body of sin might be destroyed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So putting together both the cause and the demonstration of the Resurrection which is to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not say is crucified, but is crucified with Him, so bringing baptism near to the Cross.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And on this score also it was that he said above, “We have been planted together in the likeness of His Death that the body of sin might be destroyed,” not giving that name to this body of ours, but to all iniquity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as he calls the whole sum of wickedness the old man, thus again the wickedness which is made up of the different parts of iniquity he calls the body of that man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And that what I am saying is not mere guesswork, hearken to Paul’s own interpretation of this very thing in what comes next.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after saying, “that the body of sin might be destroyed,” he adds, “that henceforth we should not serve sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the way in which I would have it dead is not so that ye should be destroyed and die, but so that ye sin not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And as he goes on he makes this still clearer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For he that is dead,” he says, “is freed (Gr. justified) from sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This he says of every man, that as he that is dead is henceforth freed from sinning, lying as a dead body, so must he that has come up from baptism, since he has died there once for all, remain ever dead to sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If then thou hast died in baptism, remain dead, for any one that dies can sin no more; but if thou sinnest, thou marrest God’s gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After requiring of us then heroism (Gr. philosophy) of this degree, he presently brings in the crown also, in these words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Now if we be dead with Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And indeed even before the crown, this is in itself the greater crown, the partaking with our Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he says, I give even another reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Of what kind is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is life eternal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “we believe,” he says, “that we shall also live with Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And whence is this clear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “That Christ being raised from the dead, dieth no more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And notice again his undauntedness, and how he makes the thing good from opposite grounds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then it was likely that some would feel perplexed at the Cross and the Death, he shows that this very thing is a ground for feeling confident henceforward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For suppose not, he says, because He once died, that He is mortal, for this is the very reason of His being immortal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For His death hath been the death of death, and because He did die, He therefore doth not die.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even that death

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “He died unto sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What does “unto sin” mean?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It means that He was not subject even to that one, but for our sin, that He might destroy it, and cut away its sinews and all its power, therefore He died.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do you see how he affrighteth them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if He does not die again, then there is no second laver, then do thou keep from all inclinableness to sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For all this he says to make a stand against the “let us do evil that good may come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us remain in sin that grace may abound.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: To take away this conception then, root and branch, it is, that he sets down all this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But in that “He liveth, He liveth unto God,” he says,—that is, unchangeably, so that death hath no more any dominion over Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if it was not through any liability to it that He died the former death, save only for the sin of others, much less will He die again now that He hath done that sin away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he says in the Epistle to the Hebrews also, “But now once,” he says, “in the end of the world hath He appeared to put away sin by the Sacrifice of Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And as it is appointed unto men once to die, and after that the judgment; so Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many, and unto them that look for Him shall He appear the second time without sin unto salvation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he both points out the power of the life that is according to God, and also the strength of sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For with regard to the life according to God, he showeth that Christ shall die no more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: With regard to sin, that if it brought about the death even of the Sinless, how can it do otherwise than be the ruin of those that are subject to it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And then as he had discoursed about His life; that none might say, What hath that which you have been saying to do with us? he adds,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Likewise reckon ye also yourselves to be dead indeed unto sin, but alive unto God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He well says, “reckon,” because there is no setting that, which he is speaking of, before the eyes as yet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what are we to reckon? one may ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That we “are dead unto sin, but alive unto God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In Jesus Christ our Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he that so liveth will lay hold of every virtue, as having Jesus Himself for his ally.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that is what, “in Christ,” means, for if He raised them when dead, much more when alive will He be able to keep them so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body, that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He does not say, let not the flesh live or act, but, “let not sin reign,” for He came not to destroy our nature, but to set our free choice aright.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then to show that it is not through any force or necessity that we are held down by iniquity, but willingly, he does not say, let it not tyrannize, a word that would imply a necessity, but let it not reign.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is absurd for those who are being conducted to the kingdom of heaven to have sin empress over them, and for those who are called to reign with Christ to choose to be the captives of sin, as though one should hurl the diadem from off his head, and choose to be the slave of a frantic woman, who came begging, and was clothed in rags.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Next since it was a heavy task to get the upper hand of sin, see how he shows it to be even easy, and how he allays the labor by saying, “in your mortal body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this shows that the struggles were but for a time, and would soon bring themselves to a close.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: At the same time he reminds us of our former evil plight, and of the root of death, as it was from this that, contrary even to its beginning, it became mortal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet it is possible even for one with a mortal body not to sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do you see the abundancy of Christ’s grace?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For Adam, though as yet he had not a mortal body, fell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But thou, who hast received one even subject to death, canst be crowned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then, is it that “sin reigns?” he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is not from any power of its own, but from thy listlessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore after saying, “let it not reign,” he also points out the mode of this reigning, by going on to say “that ye should obey it in the lusts thereof.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not honor to concede to it (i.e. to the body) all things at will, nay, it is slavery in the extreme, and the height of dishonor; for when it doth what it listeth, then is it bereft of all liberties; but when it is put under restraints, then it best keeps its own proper rank.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Neither yield ye your members as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin…but as instruments of righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The body then is indifferent between vice and virtue, as also instruments (or arms) are.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But either effect is wrought by him that useth it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As if a soldier fighting in his country’s behalf, and a robber who was arming against the inhabitants, had the same weapons for defence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the fault is not laid to the suit of armor, but to those that use it to an ill end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this one may say of the flesh too which becomes this or that owing to the mind’s decision, not owing to its own nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if it be curious after the beauty of another, the eye becomes an instrument of iniquity, not through any agency of its own (for what is of the eye, is but seeing, not seeing amiss), but through the fault of the thought which commands it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if you bridle it, it becomes an instrument of righteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thus with the tongue, thus with the hands, thus with all the other members.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he well calls sin unrighteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For by sinning a man deals unrighteously either by himself or by his neighbor, or rather by himself more than by his neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Having then led us away from wickedness, he leads us to virtue, in these words:

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But yield yourselves unto God, as those that are alive from the dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See how by his bare words he exhorts them, on that side naming “sin” and on this “God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For by showing what a difference there is between the rulers, he casts out of all excuse the soldier that leaveth God, and desireth to serve under the dominion of sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But it is not only in this way, but also by the sequel, that he establishes this; by saying, “as alive from the dead.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For by these he shows the wretchedness of the other, and the greatness of God’s gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For consider, he says, what you were, and what you have been made.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then were ye?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Dead, and ruined by a destruction which could not from any quarter be repaired.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For neither was there any one who had the power to assist you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what have ye been made out of those dead ones?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Alive with immortal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And by whom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: By the all-powerful God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Ye ought therefore to marshal yourselves under Him with as much cheerful readiness, as men would who had been made alive from being dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And your members as instruments of righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence, the body is not evil, since it may be made an arm of righteousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But by calling it an arm, he makes it clear that there is a hard warfare at hand for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for this reason we need strong armor, and also a noble spirit, and one acquainted too with the ways of this warfare; and above all we need a commander.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The Commander however is standing by, ever ready to help us, and abiding unconquerable, and has furnished us with strong arms likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Farther, we have need of a purpose of mind to handle them as should be, so that we may both obey our Commander, and take the field for our country.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Having then given us this vigorous exhortation, and reminded us of arms, and battle, and wars, see how he encourages the soldier again and cherishes his ready spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For sin shall no more have dominion over you; for ye are not under the Law, but under grace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If then sin hath no more dominion over us, why does he lay so great a charge upon them as he does in the words, “Let not sin reign in your mortal body,” and, “yield not ye your members as instruments of unrighteousness unto sin?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What does that here said mean then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He is sowing a kind of seed in this statement, which he means to develop afterwards, and to cultivate in a powerful argument.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then is this statement?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is this; that our body, before Christ’s coming, was an easy prey to the assaults of sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after death a great swarm of passions entered also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for this cause it was not lightsome for running the race of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there was no Spirit present to assist, nor any baptism of power to mortify.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But as some horse (Plato Phædr. §74) that answereth not the rein, it ran indeed, but made frequent slips, the Law meanwhile announcing what was to be done and what not, yet not conveying into those in the race anything over and above exhortation by means of words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But when Christ had come, the effort became afterwards more easy, and therefore we had a more distant goal (μείζονα τὰ σκάμματα) set us, in that the assistance we had given us was greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore also Christ saith, “Except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the Scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the Kingdom of Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this he says more clearly in the sequel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But at present he alludes here briefly to it, to show that unless we stoop down very low to it, sin will not get the better of us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is not the Law only that exhorteth us, but grace too which also remitted our former sins, and secures us against future ones.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it promised them crowns after toils, but this (i.e. grace) crowned them first, and then led them to the contest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now it seems to me that he is not signifying here the whole life of a believer, but instituting a comparison between the Baptism and the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this he says in another passage also; “The letter killeth, but the Spirit giveth life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (2 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the Law convinceth of transgression, but grace undoes transgression.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then the former by convincing establisheth sin so the latter by forgiving suffereth us not to be under sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so thou art in two ways set free from this thraldom; both in thy not being under the Law, and in thy enjoying grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After then he had by these words given the hearer a breathing time, he again furnishes him a safeguard, by introducing an exhortation in reply to an objection, and by saying as follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “What then? shall we sin, because we are not under the Law, but under grace?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: God forbid.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So he first adopted a form of adjuration, because it was an absurd thing he had named.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And then he makes his discourse pass on to exhortation, and shows the great facility of the struggle, in the following words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Know ye not, that to whom ye yield yourselves servants to obey, his servants ye are to whom ye obey; whether of sin unto death, or of obedience unto righteousness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I do not, he would say, mention hell as yet, nor that great (ms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Bodl. long) punishment, but the shame it is in this world, when ye become slaves, and slaves of your own accord too, and sin’s slaves, and when the wages are such as a second death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if before baptism, it wrought death of the body, and the wound required so great attendance, that the Lord of all came down to die, and so put a stop to the evil; if after so great a gift, and so great liberty, it seize thee again, while thou bendest down under it willingly, what is there that it may not do?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do not then run into such a pit, or willingly give thyself up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For in the case of wars, soldiers are often given up even against their will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But in this case, unless thou desertest of thyself, there is no one who will get the better of thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Having then tried to shame them by a sense of duty, he alarms them also by the rewards, and lays before them the wages of both; righteousness, and death, and that a death not like the former, but far worse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if Christ is to die no more, who is to do away with death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: We must then be punished, and have vengeance taken upon us forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For a death preceptible to the senses is not still to come in this case, as in the former, which gives the body rest, and separates it from the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For the last enemy, death, is destroyed” (1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 26), whence the punishment will be deathless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But not to them that obey, for righteousness, and the blessings springing from it, will be their rewards.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “But God be thanked, that ye were the servants of sin, but ye have obeyed from the heart that form of doctrine which was delivered unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Lit. “into which ye were delivered.”)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: After shaming them by the slavery, after alarming them by the rewards, and so exhorting them, he again rights them by calling the benefits to mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For by these he shows that they were great evils from which they were freed, and that not by any labors of their own, and that things henceforth would be more manageable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Just as any one who has rescued a captive from a cruel tyrant, and advises him not to run away back to him, reminds him of his grievous thraldom; so does Paul set the evils passed away most emphatically before us, by giving thanks to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was no human power that could set us free from all those evils, but, “thanks be to God,” who was willing and able to do such great things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he well says, “Ye have obeyed from the heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Ye were neither forced nor pressed, but ye came over of your own accord, with willing mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now this is like one that praises and rebukes at once.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after having willingly come, and not having had any necessity to undergo, what allowance can you claim, or what excuse can you make, if you run away back to your former estate?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Next that you may learn that it came not of your own willing temper only, but the whole of it of God’s grace also, after saying, “Ye have obeyed from the heart,” he adds, “that form of doctrine which was delivered you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the obedience from the heart shows the free will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the being delivered, hints the assistance from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But what is the form of doctrine?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is living aright, and in conformity with the best conversation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Being then made free from sin, ye became the servants of righteousness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: There are two gifts of God which he here points out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The “freeing from sin,” and also the “making them servants to righteousness,” which is better than any freedom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For God hath done the same as if a person were to take an orphan, who had been carried away by savages into their own country, and were not only to free him from captivity, but were to set a kind father over him, and bring him to very great dignity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this has been done in our case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was not our old evils alone that He freed us from, since He even led us to the life of angels, and paved the way for us to the best conversation, handing us over to the safe keeping of righteousness, and killing our former evils, and deadening the old man, and leading us to an immortal life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us then continue living this life; for many of those who seem to breathe and to walk about are in a more wretched plight than the dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there are different kinds of deadness; and one there is of the body, according to which Abraham was dead, and still was not dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “God,” He says, “is not a God of the dead, but of the living.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Matt. xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Another is of the soul which Christ alludes to when He says, “Let the dead bury their dead.” (ib. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Another, which is even the subject of praise, which is brought about by religion (φιλοσοφίας), of which Paul saith, “Mortify your members which are upon the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Another, which is the cause even of this, the one which takes place in baptism.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For our old man,” he says, “has been crucified” (ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 6), that is, has been deadened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then we know this, let us flee from the deadness by which, even though alive, we die.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And let us not be afraid of that with which common death comes on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the other two, whereof one is blissful, having been given by God, the other praiseworthy (cf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 12), which is accomplished by ourselves together with God, let us both choose and be emulous of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And of those two, one doth David pronounce blessed, when he says, “Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven” (Ps. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 1); and the other, Paul holds in admiration, saying, and writing to the Galatians, “They that be Christ’s have crucified the flesh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But of the other couple, one Christ declares to be easy to hold in contempt, when He says, “Fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul:” and the other fearful, for, “Fear” (He says) “Him that is able to destroy both body and soul in hell.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And therefore let us flee from this, and choose that deadness which is held blessed and admirable; that of the other two, we may escape the one and not fear the other: for it is not the least good to us to see the sun, and to eat and drink, unless the life of good words be with us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what would be the advantage, pray, of a king dressed in a purple robe and possessed of arms, but without a single subject, and exposed to all that had a mind to attack and insult him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In like manner it will be no advantage to a Christian to have faith, and the gift of baptism, and yet be open to all the passions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In that way the disgrace will be greater, and the shame more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as such an one having the diadem and purple is so far from gaining by this dress any honor to himself, that he even does disgrace to that by his own shame: so the believer also, who leadeth a corrupt life, is so far from becoming, as such, an object of respect, that he is only the more one of scorn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For as many,” it says, “as sinned without law, shall also perish without law; and as many as have sinned in the law, shall be judged by the law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in the Epistle to the Hebrews, he says, “He that despised Moses’ law died without mercy under two or three witnesses: of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who had trodden under foot the Son of God?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For I placed (He might say) all the passions in subjection to thee by baptism.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then comes it that thou hast disgraced so great a gift, and hast become one thing instead of another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I have killed and buried thy former transgressions, like worms—how is it that thou hast bred others?—for sins are worse than worms, since these do harm to the body, those to the soul; and those make the more offensive stench.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet we perceive it not, and so we are at no pains to purge them out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thus the drunkard knows not how disgustful the stale wine is, but he that is not drunken has a distinct perception of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So with sins also, he that lives soberly knows thoroughly that other mire, and the stain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But he that gives himself up to wickedness, like a man made drowsy with drunkenness, does not even know the very fact that he is ill.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is the most grievous part of vice, that it does not allow those who fall into it even to see the greatness of their own bane, but as they lie in the mire, they think they are enjoying perfumes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so they have not even the power of getting free, but when full of worms, like men that pride themselves in precious stones, so do they exult in these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And for this reason they have not so much as the will to kill them, but they even nourish these up, and multiply them in themselves, until they send them on to the worms of the world to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For these are providers for those, and are not only providers, but even the fathers of those that never die; as it says, “their worm shall not die.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: These kindle the hell which never extinguishes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: To prevent this from happening then, let us do away with this fountain of evil, and extinguish the furnace, and let us draw up the root of wickedness from beneath, since you will do no good by cutting the tree off from above, if the root remains below, and sends up fresh shoots of the same kind again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What then is the root of the evils?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Learn from the good husbandman (i.e. St. Paul 1 Cor. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 6–9), who has an accurate knowledge of such things, and tends the spiritual vine and cultivates the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now what does he say is the cause of all the evils?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The love of money.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the “love of money is the root of all evils.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence come fightings, and enmities and wars; hence emulations, and railings, and suspicions, and insults; hence murders, and thefts, and violations of sepulchres.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Through this, not cities and countries only, but roads and habitable and inhabitable parts, and mountains, and groves, and hills, and, in a word, all places are filled with blood and murder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And not even from the sea has this evil withdrawn, but even there also with great fury hath it revelled, since pirates beset it on all sides, thus devising a new mode of robbery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Through this have the laws of nature been subverted, and the claims of relationship set aside, and the laws of piety itself broken through.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the thraldom of money hath armed, not against the living only, but even against the departed too, the right hands of such men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And at death even, they make no truce with them, but bursting open the sepulchres, they put forth their impious hands even against dead bodies, and not even him that hath let go of life will they suffer to be let go from their plotting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And all the evils that you may find, whether in the house or in the market-place, or in the courts of law, or in the senate, or in the king’s palace, or in any other place whatsoever, it is from this that you will find they all spring.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this evil it is, this assuredly, which fills all places with blood and murder, this lights up the flame of hell, this makes cities as wretchedly off as a wilderness, yea, even much worse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For those that beset the high roads, one can easily be on one’s guard against, as not being always upon attack.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But they who in the midst of cities imitate them are so much the worse than them, in that these are harder to guard against, and dare to do openly what the others do with secrecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For those laws, which have been made with a view to stopping their iniquity, they draw even into alliance and fill the cities with this kind of murders and pollutions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is it not murder, pray, and worse than murder, to hand the poor man over to famine, and to cast him into prison, and to expose him not to famine only, but to tortures too, and to countless acts of insolence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even if you do not do these things yourself to him, yet you are the occasion of their being done, you do them more than the ministers who execute them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The murderer plunges his sword into a man at once, and after giving him pain for a short time, he does not carry the torture any farther.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But do you who by your calumnies, by your harassings, by your plottings, make light darkess to him, and set him upon desiring death ten thousand times over, consider how many deaths you perpetrate instead of one only?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is worse than all, you plunder and are grasping, not impelled to it by poverty, without any hunger to necessitate you, but that your horse’s bridle may be spattered over with gold enough, or the ceiling of your house, or the capitals of your pillars.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what hell is there that this conduct would not deserve, when it is a brother, and one that has shared with yourself in blessings unutterable, and has been so highly honored by the Lord, whom you, in order that you may deck out stones, and floors, and the bodies of animals with neither reason, nor perception of these ornaments, are casting into countless calamities?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And your dog is well attended too, while man, or rather Christ, for the sake of the hound, and all these things I have named, is straitened with extreme hunger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What can be worse than such confusion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What more grievous than such lawlessness as this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What streams of fire will be enough for such a soul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He that was made in the Image of God stands in unseemly plight, through thy inhumanity; but the faces of the mules that draw thy wife glisten with gold in abundance, as do the skins and woods which compose that canopy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if it is a seat that is to be made, or a footstool, they are all made of gold and silver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the member of Christ, for whom also He came hither from Heaven, and shed His precious Blood, does not even enjoy the food that is necessary for him, owing to thy rapaciousness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the couches are mantled with silver on every side, while the bodies of the saints are deprived even of necessary clothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And to thee Christ is less precious than anything else, servants, or mules, or couch, or chair, or footstool; for I pass over furniture of still meaner use than these, leaving it to you to know of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if thou art shocked at hearing this, stand aloof from doing it, and then the words spoken will not harm thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Stand aloof, and cease from this madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For plain madness it is, such eagerness about these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore letting go of these things, let us look up, late as it is, towards Heaven, and let us call to mind the Day which is coming, let us bethink ourselves of that awful tribunal, and the exact accounts, and the sentence incorruptible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us consider that God, who sees all these things, sends no lightnings from Heaven; and yet what is done deserves not thunderbolts merely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet He neither doth this, nor doth He let the sea loose upon us, nor doth He burst the earth in twain, He quencheth not the sun, nor doth He hurl the heaven with its stars upon us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He doth not move aught from its place, but suffereth them to hold their course, and the whole creation to minister to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Pondering all this then, let us be awestruck with the greatness of His love toward man, and let us return to that noble origin which belongs to us, since at present certainly we are in no better plight than the creatures without reason, but even in a much worse one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For they do love their kin, and need but the community of nature to cause affection towards each other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But thou who besides nature hast countless causes to draw thee together and attach thee to the member of thyself; the being honored with the Word, the partaking in one religion, the sharing in countless blessings; art become of wilder nature than they, by displaying so much carefulness about profitless things, and leaving the Temples of God to perish in hunger and nakedness, and often surrounding them also with a thousand evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if it is from love of glory that you do these things, it is much more binding on you to show your brother attention, than your horse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the better the creature that enjoys the act of kindness, the brighter the crown that is woven for such carefulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since now while thou fallest into the contrary of all this, thou pullest upon thyself accusers without number, yet perceivest it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For who is there that will not speak ill of thee? who that will not indite thee as guilty of the greatest atrocity and misanthrophy, when he sees that thou disregardest the human race, and settest that of senseless creatures above men, and besides senseless creatures, even the furniture of thy house?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hast thou not heard the Apostles say, that they who first received the word sold both “houses and lands” (Acts iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 34), that they might support the brethren? but you plunder both houses and lands, that you may adorn a horse or wood-work, or skins, or walls, or a pavement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is worse is, that it is not men only, but women too are afflicted with this madness, and urge their husbands to this empty sort of pains, by forcing them to lay out their money upon anything rather than the necessary things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And if any one accuse them for this, they are practised with a defence, itself loaded with much to be accused.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For both the one and the other are done at once, says one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What say you? are you not afraid to utter such a thing, and to set the same store by horses and mules and couches and footstools, as by Christ an hungered?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather not even comparing them at all, but giving the larger share to these, and to Him meting out with difficulty a scant share?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Dost thou not know that all belongs to Him, both thou and thine?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Dost thou not know that He fashioned thy body, as well as gave thee a soul, and apportioned thee the whole world? but thou art not for giving a little recompense to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if thou lettest a little hut, thou requirest the rent with the utmost rigor, and though reaping the whole of His creation, and dwelling in so wide a world, thou hast not courage to lay down even a little rent, but has given up to vainglory thyself and all thou hast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is that whereof all these things come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: The horse is none the better above his natural excellence for having this ornament, neither yet is the person mounted upon him, for sometimes he is only in the less esteem for it; since many neglect the rider and turn their eyes to the horse’s ornaments, and to the attendants behind and before, and to the fan-bearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the man, who is lackeyed by these, they hate and turn their heads from, as a common enemy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this does not happen when thou adornest thy soul, for then men, and angels, and the Lord of angels, all weave thee a crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so, if thou art in love with glory, stand aloof from the things which thou art now doing, and show thy taste not in thy house, but in thy soul, that thou mayest become brilliant and conspicuous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For now nothing can be more cheap than thou art, with thy soul unfurnished, and but the handsomeness of thy house for a screen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if thou art impatient of hearing me speak in this way, listen to what one of those that are without did, and at all events be shamed by their philosophy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is said that a certain one of them, who went into a palace that shone with gold in abundance, and glistened with the great beauty of the marbles and the columns, when he saw the floor strewed with carpets in all directions, spat in the face of the master of the house, and when found fault with for it said, that since there was no other part of the house where he could do this, he was obliged to do this affront to his face.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See how ridiculous a man is, who displays his taste in exteriors, and how little he is in the eyes of all reasonable men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And with good reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if a person were to leave thy wife to be clad in rags, and to be neglected, and clothed thy maid-servants with brilliant dresses, thou wouldest not bear it meekly, but wouldest be exasperated, and say that it was insulting in the extreme.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Reason then in this way about your soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: When you display your taste in walls then, and pavement, and furniture, and other things of the kind, and do not give liberally in alms, or practise the other parts of a religious life (φιλοσοφίαν); you do nothing less than this, or rather what is worse than this by far.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the difference between servant and mistress is nothing, but between soul and flesh, there is a great disparity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if it be so with the flesh, much more is it with a house or a couch or a footstool.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What kind of excuse then dost thou deserve, who puttest silver on all these, but for it hast no regard, though it be covered with filthy rags, squalid, hungry, and full of wounds, torn by hounds unnumbered (Luke xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 20, 21); and after all this fanciest that thou shalt get thee glory by displaying thy taste in externals wound about thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is the very height of phrenzy, while ridiculed, reproached, disgraced, dishonored, and falling into the severest punishment, still to be vain of these things!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore, I beseech you, laying all this to heart, let us become sober-minded, late as it is, and become our own masters, and transfer this adorning from outward things to our souls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For so it will abide safe from spoiling, and will make us equal to the angels, and will entertain us with unaltering good, which may we all attain by the grace and love toward man, etc.
